about: STAGING — not loaded by the site. Baseline = production post_thread_edges.json. Editorial tranches include rationale (optional_review marks edges to drop unless later batches reinforce). Batches 1–2 as before; Batch 3 = 2018-03-21–2018-05-22. Validate: node scripts/validate-post-thread-edges.mjs _data/post_thread_edges.staging.json

edges.rationale: The first essay’s non-supernatural God-talk and break with Anglican ministry widens, months later, into an explicit analysis of how neo-medieval theology and seminary formation reinscribe hierarchy and violence.

The closing claim that non-realist Christian language fits Unitarian culture sets up the later address comparing how Unitarians and Quakers actually embody dissenting Christian roots in worship and speech.
Hewett’s abstract model of Unitarian pulls is given flesh by a concrete portrait of dissenting family life, money, and conscience in the milieu that formed a famous liberal household.
The memorial returns directly to the same Hewett diagram criticised in the earlier sermon, revising—not replacing—the argument in light of Hewett’s late insistence on centred stories and Jesus.
Buddhist analysis of craving and compulsive mental noise clarifies what is spiritually at stake when the Quaker essay treats communal silence as formation, not mere rest.

After locating Unitarianism beside Quakerism in a shared radical-Protestant story, the lineage essay names Neoplatonist, hermetic, and related currents that often sit unnamed in the same liberal chapels.
The Time-cover argument that public ‘truth’ is narrated and tribal pairs naturally with the claim that biography never delivers a life transparently but always mounts an interested case.

Ted-and-Sylvia life-writing as partial, morally charged interpretation foreshadows the redemption sermon’s insistence that even the preacher offers one situated reading of Isaiah and art, not a final one.
The later sermon explicitly picks up the fourfold, elemental pattern introduced in the alchemy address and reworks it through Potter houses, Jungian types, and Marian earth-symbolism.
Newton-as-alchemist and inner transformation supply a sermon-level entry point for the later essay’s wider map of how philosophy, religion, and occult practice braid in Western esotericism.
After reframing creedal descent through Jonah, shadow, and return, the next sermon advances the same Holy Week arc into Jerusalem’s Golden Gate as public, temple-challenging kingship.

The Palm Sunday reading of passion-week symbolism leads straight into Easter’s question of how resurrection language evolves from Paul’s ambiguity to John’s ark-of-the-covenant tableau and inner/outer truth.

The Easter sermon’s distinction between verifiable outer truth and mythic inner truth sets up James’s pragmatic claim that spiritually salient beliefs may be ‘true’ in lived interior worlds without answering geophysical tests.

Fight-or-flight tightening that cannot reassess a cucumber prefigures Carve Space’s argument that unexamined worldviews and ‘principles’ shrink habitable inner territory before any outward activism can stay humane.

McIntosh/Carmichael spiritual activism and nonviolent cheek-turning supply a theoretical frame for Fairfax’s anniversary question of what inward habitation this particular meeting house is meant to school.
The later address explicitly cites the apocalypse sermon while using Childish Gambino’s video as a cultural case of how catastrophic imagination rearranges what counts as real for a community.

James’s insistence that each psyche is its own world of meaning clarifies why Malick’s transcendent guarantor-God can feel simultaneously compelling on screen and theologically alien from another interior economy.
